title: Pseva japonica
intro:
I couldn't find any information on the plant Pseva japonica. However, based on the name and description, it might be related to Pueraria montana var. lobata, commonly known as Kudzu or Pueraria. 

Here is a paragraph about Pueraria montana var. lobata:

Pueraria montana var. lobata, commonly known as Kudzu, is a type of flowering plant native to East Asia. The health benefits of Pueraria montana var. lobata include its potential to help lower blood sugar levels, reduce inflammation, and improve cardiovascular health. The bioactive constituents of this plant include isoflavones, such as daidzein and genistein, which have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Pueraria montana var. lobata include teas, capsules, and tinctures, which are often used to support blood sugar control and cardiovascular health.

parts: Pseva japonica is commonly known as the Japanese Pseva. Pseva japonica has various medicinal parts including its roots and leaves, which are used to treat various health conditions. The leaves and roots of the plant are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them useful in treating fever, colds, and other respiratory issues. In traditional medicine, Pseva japonica is also used to treat digestive problems and skin conditions. The exact list of its medicinal parts may vary depending on the source and traditional practices.
preparations: Pseva japonica, also known as Japanese boxwood, has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, particularly in Asian countries. Various herbal preparations are made from its leaves, stems, and roots, which are believed to possess medicinal properties. These preparations are often used to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues.
